content:
# Diagenesis

**Summary**: The chemical, physical, and biological changes in sediment or rock after deposition.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1996-jarad|Jarad, 1996]] identified diagenesis as a factor influencing the petrographic and geochemical characteristics of phosphorite grains.

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] evaluated the diagenetic factors influencing the formation of sedimentary apatite.

- [[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001]] investigated diagenesis as the primary control on reservoir quality.

- [[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002]] analyzed diagenesis to determine its impact on the reservoir quality of the Unayzah Formation.

- [[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992]] not used directly as a primary focus of this thesis.

- [[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988]] investigated the diagenetic processes in the carbonate sequences.

- [[2006-al-enezi|Al-Enezi, 2006]] investigated the lithological characteristics of the Dam Formation.

- [[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010]] evaluated diagenesis to identify sequence boundaries and paleo-environmental conditions.

- [[2011-al-masrahy|AL-MASRAHY, 2011]] not used in this study

- [[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011]] evaluated the diagenetic impact on carbonate textures.

- [[2012-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2012]] assessed the impact of diagenesis on source rock quality.

- [[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013]] investigated diagenetic processes including micritization, dissolution, and dolomitization.

- [[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014]] investigated the diagenetic processes affecting the Sarah Formation's porosity and permeability.

- [[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014]] analyzed diagenesis to determine its impact on porosity and permeability.

- [[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014]] evaluated diagenesis to understand the evolution of porosity.

- [[2014-sami|Sami, 2014]] investigated the role of diagenesis in creating reservoir heterogeneity.

- [[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014]] investigated diagenetic processes like cementation in the Siq Sandstone.

- [[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015]] investigated the diagenetic evolution of carbonate platforms.

- [[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015]] evaluated diagenesis as a key factor for reservoir quality.

- [[2015-ali|Ali, 2015]] investigated the diagenesis of the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016]] analyzed diagenetic processes including cementation and authigenic clay growth.

- [[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016]] investigated diagenesis, specifically dolomitization, in the mud layers.

- [[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016]] analyzed the impact of diagenesis on reservoir quality in the Khuff Formation.

- [[2016-salih|Salih, 2016]] investigated the impact of diagenetic processes like dolomitization and cementation on porosity.

- [[2016-radwan|Radwan, 2016]] investigated diagenetic processes of calcitization and chertification.

- [[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016]] analyzed the diagenetic impact on reservoir quality.

- [[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017]] analyzed the impact of diagenesis on carbonate mud rocks.

- [[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017]] investigated the diagenetic characteristics of the Sarah Formation.

- [[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017]] analyzed the diagenetic features of the TMF.

- [[2017-adam|Adam, 2017]] analyzed diagenesis to determine its impact on reservoir quality and heterogeneity.

- [[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017]] analyzed diagenesis to understand its impact on reservoir quality.

- [[2017-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2017]] evaluated the impact of diagenesis on reservoir quality.

- [[2018-butt|Butt, 2018]] analyzed diagenesis to determine the impact on reservoir quality.

- [[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018]] evaluated diagenetic processes like dolomitization and cementation to assess their impact on reservoir properties.

- [[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020]] analyzed diagenesis to determine its impact on reservoir quality.

- [[2020-islam|Islam, 2020]] evaluated the diagenetic controls on reservoir quality.

- [[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020]] Not stated in source

- [[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021]] investigated diagenetic processes in the Tuwaiq Mountain Formation.

- [[2021-ahmed|Ahmed, 2021]] investigated the diagenetic evolution of the Marrat Formation including dissolution and cementation.

- [[2021-alqarni|Alqarni, 2021]] evaluated diagenesis including cementation and compaction to assess reservoir quality.

- [[2021-alabyadh|Alabyadh, 2021]] investigated diagenesis to determine the history of carbonate formation.

- [[2022-khan|Khan, 2022]] analyzed the diagenesis of the studied core samples.

- [[2022-samarkin|Samarkin, 2022]] investigated via mineralogical analysis to see how chemicals altered the carbonate matrix.

- [[2022-stiawan|Stiawan, 2022]] analyzed the impact of diagenetic processes on carbonate porosity.

- [[2022-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2022]] identified diagenetic and non-diagenetic microfacies.

- [[2022-salih|Salih, 2022]] investigated the impact of diagenesis on pore types and sonic velocity.

- [[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022]] investigated diagenetic features like encrustation and cementation.

- [[2022-al-sadah|Al-Sadah, 2022]] investigated the diagenetic features of the carbonate rocks.

- [[2022-babker|Babker, 2022]] investigated diagenetic overprints on the Upper Khartam Member.

- [[2023-butt|Butt, 2023]] analyzed diagenesis to determine its impact on reservoir quality.

- [[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]] Not used in this study.

- [[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024]] Not stated in source

- [[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024]] identified diagenesis as the primary driver for the observed universality of rock property trends.

- [[2024-allam|Allam, 2024]] investigated the role of diagenetic processes on reservoir quality.

- [[2024-alsadah|Alsadah, 2024]] considered the impact of diagenesis on foraminiferal shell chemistry.

- [[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]] considered diagenesis as a factor influencing mineralogy and porosity.

- [[2024-malik|Malik, 2024]] identified diagenetic processes such as chertification and dolomitization.

- [[2024-fahmi|Fahmi, 2024]] Not stated in source

- [[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024]] N/A

- [[2024-muharrag|Muharrag, 2024]] investigated diagenesis to understand the difference in petrophysical properties below the sequence boundary.

- [[2025-refaat|Refaat, 2025]] evaluated the diagenetic modifications within burrow fillings.

- [[1989-abdou|Abdou, 1989]] identified diagenetic features to support environmental interpretation.

- [[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992]] analyzed diagenesis to determine its impact on reservoir porosity.

- [[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999]] investigated the diagenetic history of the Wajid Sandstone.

- [[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985]] identified post-depositional diagenetic changes in the carbonate rocks.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[1996-jarad|Jarad, 1996]] Petrographic analysis

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Diagenesis included reworking, leaching, and recrystallization. ([[1996-jarad|Jarad, 1996, p. 53]])

- Suggested a biochemical origin for the deposits rather than purely abiogenic processes. ([[1996-matter|Matter, 1996, p. 26]])

- Diagenetic processes include compaction, cementation, and dissolution (secondary pore generation). ([[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001, p. 18]])

- Key stages included compaction, cementation (calcite, clay, quartz), and dissolution-induced secondary porosity. ([[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002, p. 23]])

- Not stated in source ([[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992]])

- Diagenetic processes included cementation, micritization, and dissolution of aragonitic components. ([[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988, p. 13]])

- Not stated in source ([[2006-al-enezi|Al-Enezi, 2006]])

- Meteoric cements and karst features were used as primary indicators of subaerial exposure and unconformities. ([[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010, p. 85]])

- Not stated in source ([[2011-al-masrahy|AL-MASRAHY, 2011]])

- Identified Dolomitic Breccia as a result of dissolution of interbedded evaporites during transgressions. ([[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011, p. 53]])

- Not stated in source ([[2012-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2012]])

- Identified multiple stages of diagenesis including early and late dolomitization. ([[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013, p. 74]])

- Iron cementation and kaolinite formation were key diagenetic factors impacting petrophysical properties. ([[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014, p. 78]])

- Kaolinite cementation and quartz overgrowth are the primary diagenetic factors affecting reservoir quality. ([[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014, p. 109]])

- Dolomitization significantly enhanced the overall porosity in the graded mudstone to packstone facies. ([[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014, p. 132]])

- Diagenesis is one of the two main controls on reservoir heterogeneity at the outcrop scale. ([[2014-sami|Sami, 2014, p. 18]])

- Original calcite cement was replaced by microcrystalline quartz in the Siq Sandstone. ([[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014, p. 83]])

- Diagenetic processes included micritization, cementation, dissolution, neomorphism, and dolomitization. ([[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015, p. 67]])

- Reservoir quality is driven by both depositional facies and diagenesis (e.g., dolomitization). ([[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015, p. 45]])

- Diagenetic controls are present and contribute to the variability of porosity and permeability. ([[2015-ali|Ali, 2015, p. 25]])

- Cementation by calcite and iron oxide were primary factors affecting porosity and permeability. ([[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016, p. 14]])

- Dolomite was found to be the main mineral content in mudstone when interbedded with evaporites due to Mg-rich fluids. ([[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016, p. 122]])

- Key early-stage processes include dolomitization, selective dissolution, and anhydrite-calcite cementation. ([[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016, p. 47]])

- Dolomitization was identified as a key process that occurred in grainstone facies underlying mudstone-evaporite beds. ([[2016-salih|Salih, 2016, p. 52]])

- Two diagenetic processes (chertification and calcitization) distinguish the Jurassic upper Tuwaiq Mountain Formation. ([[2016-radwan|Radwan, 2016, p. 14]])

- Meteoric diagenesis and dolomitization were the primary drivers of porosity enhancement and preservation. ([[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016, p. 48]])

- Diagenetic processes influenced the mineralogical composition and porosity. ([[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017, p. 160]])

- Feldspar dissolution and silica overgrowth were key diagenetic processes affecting reservoir quality. ([[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017, p. 20]])

- Diagenetic processes include dissolution, dolomitization, and cementation which impact porosity. ([[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017, p. 100]])

- Diagenetic processes were identified as key factors influencing porosity and permeability. ([[2017-adam|Adam, 2017, p. 13]])

- Identified meteoric dissolution and dolomitization as key diagenetic processes. ([[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017, p. 102]])

- Diagenetic processes were identified as a key factor affecting porosity and permeability. ([[2017-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2017, p. 168]])

- Major diagenetic processes included mechanical compaction, carbonate cementation, and pyrite/hematite cementation. ([[2018-butt|Butt, 2018, p. 140]])

- Dedolomitization in burrowed wackestone led to higher porosity and permeability. ([[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018, p. 17]])

- Identified several key processes including micritization and dissolution that significantly altered porosity. ([[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020, p. 109]])

- Key diagenetic processes included compaction, dissolution, cementation, and grain coating. ([[2020-islam|Islam, 2020, p. 120]])

- Not stated in source ([[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020]])

- Main types included cementation, recrystallization, silicification, and dolomitization. ([[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021, p. 38]])

- Iron oxide cementation was a major diagenetic process that filled moldic pores and microfractures. ([[2021-ahmed|Ahmed, 2021, p. 63]])

- Identified carbonate and silica cements as primary factors for pore-space reduction. ([[2021-alqarni|Alqarni, 2021, p. 83]])

- Identified key stages including microbial micritization, dolomitization, and dedolomitization. ([[2021-alabyadh|Alabyadh, 2021, p. 102]])

- Identified significant dolomitization and cementation processes affecting porosity and permeability. ([[2022-khan|Khan, 2022, p. 117]])

- NaF treatment led to the formation of fluorite (CaF2). ([[2022-samarkin|Samarkin, 2022, p. 12]])

- Diagenetic features included micritization, dissolution, dolomitization (FP/NFP), burial/compaction, fracturing, and dedolomitization. ([[2022-stiawan|Stiawan, 2022, p. 43]])

- Identified interparticle pores formed by various minerals like kaolinite and illite during burial. ([[2022-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2022, p. 39]])

- Cementation of oomolds by calcite significantly reduced pore aspect ratio while only slightly increasing velocity. ([[2022-salih|Salih, 2022, p. 60]])

- Encrustation by oysters and microbial crusts was observed on coral heads in F7. ([[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022, p. 61]])

- Not stated in source ([[2022-al-sadah|Al-Sadah, 2022]])

- This similarity enables the study of the lateral variability of the facies and their diagenetic overprints. ([[2022-babker|Babker, 2022, p. 86]])

- Diagenetic characterization showed that reservoir quality is mainly controlled by depositional characteristics, with diagenesis playing a secondary role. ([[2023-butt|Butt, 2023, p. 30]])

- Not stated in source ([[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]])

- Not stated in source ([[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024]])

- Diagenetic processes were found to be a universal trend across different geographic locations. ([[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024, p. 56]])

- Early-stage burial diagenesis was indicated by a shift toward more negative δ18O values. ([[2024-allam|Allam, 2024, p. 11]])

- Diagenesis is identified as a key factor that can alter the chemical composition of foram shells, impacting proxy reliability. ([[2024-alsadah|Alsadah, 2024, p. 67]])

- Different diagenetic processes produce different chemistry, mineralogy, and thus porosity. ([[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024, p. 18]])

- Chertification was determined to be of diagenetic origin in F5. ([[2024-malik|Malik, 2024, p. 49]])

- Not stated in source ([[2024-fahmi|Fahmi, 2024]])

- N/A ([[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024]])

- Stromatolites below the SB were modified by diagenetic processes, leading to more uniform pore systems. ([[2024-muharrag|Muharrag, 2024, p. 32]])

- Diagenetic processes such as dissolution of dolomite rhombs and cementation significantly impacted porosity levels. ([[2025-refaat|Refaat, 2025, p. 34]])

- Common features included recrystallization, iron staining, and silicification. ([[1989-abdou|Abdou, 1989, p. 30]])

- Cementation and micritization were pore-reducing, while dissolution and dolomitization were pore-enlarging. ([[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992, p. 18]])

- Identified compaction, cementation (calcite), and dissolution as primary diagenetic processes. ([[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999, p. 52]])

- Evidence suggests magnesite was deposited followed by some post-depositional (diagenetic) changes. ([[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985, p. 23]])
